critique.summary: The article describes a game called Hostile Volume, where players must set and maintain the volume at exactly 25% to progress through various levels. The game features intentionally poor user interface design and humorous elements that critique typical gaming experiences.
mainPoints: Players must hold the volume at 25% for 3 seconds to progress.
The game includes various levels with unique challenges related to volume control.
It features a satirical take on user interface design and gaming mechanics.
critique.truthfulness: The claims made in the article about the gameplay mechanics and the design philosophy of Hostile Volume appear to be accurate based on the provided content. However, the humorous and exaggerated descriptions of player experiences may not reflect the actual reception of the game.
considerations: The article does not provide any information on the game's reception or reviews from players.
There is no mention of the game's development background or the developers' intentions.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the challenges and humor associated with intentionally poor volume control UIs in a game. Users share personal experiences and insights about similar issues in real-world applications.

comments.author: dsmason321
comments.text: Level 17 is NOT bugged. The slider is backward and the volume nonresponsive. Its a planned feature.
comments.analysis: This comment provides a clear and accurate explanation of a specific game feature, addressing a common misconception.

comments.author: jimkleiber
comments.text:
Got an error on Level 17, just a heads up.

Love the game, btw.
comments.analysis: While the user reports an issue, they also express enjoyment of the game, making the comment both informative and positive.

comments.author: graypegg
comments.text: This is not an issue at all, but when ever I come across something like it, I like to poke at the frontend in dev tools...
comments.analysis: The comment demonstrates technical knowledge and offers a solution, though it downplays the frustrations others may feel.

comments.author: TheLNL
comments.text: Finished the game. It was fun to play. I got stuck for a while on the opposite level where the display doesn't update...
comments.analysis: This comment shares a personal experience and encourages others, though it lacks specific details about the issues faced.

comments.author: Retr0id
comments.text: There are two types of volume slider I've encountered thus far, "too logarithmic", and "not logarithmic enough".
comments.analysis: The comment provides a general observation about volume sliders, but it lacks direct relevance to the game discussed.

comments.author: jonathanlydall
comments.text: My favourite bad volume control was in Real Player around 1997 where changing the volume in the application actually changed...
comments.analysis: While nostalgic and relevant, this comment does not directly address the current game, making it less impactful.

comments.author: Pipe94
comments.text: somehow i'm amazed and annoyed at the same time
comments.analysis: This comment expresses mixed feelings but lacks detail or constructive feedback, making it less informative.

comments.author: DrSiemer
comments.text: Plenty of annoyance in here for sure. Looks like 17 cannot be finished on mobile though. Switching to desktop view resets...
comments.analysis: The comment raises a valid concern about mobile gameplay, but it could benefit from more context or solutions.

comments.author: Findecanor
comments.text:
I have encountered the rate-limited spinner (#8) and the self-resizing slider (#5) in real desktop UIs.

#3 are almost like...
comments.analysis: This comment draws parallels to real-world UIs, but it is somewhat tangential to the main discussion about the game.

comments.author: xnx
comments.text: The worst volume control UI in the world (2017): https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=27819384
comments.analysis: This comment provides a link but lacks context or relevance to the current discussion, making it less valuable.

comments.author: danjl
comments.text: ...and, of course, there's really no need for a volume control in any app, since there's already a system volume...
comments.analysis: While the comment raises a valid point, it dismisses the game's premise and comes off as somewhat dismissive.

comments.author: wild_pointer
comments.text: Hilarious, some of them are easy with the keyboard
comments.analysis: The comment is lighthearted but lacks depth or specific insights about the game mechanics.

comments.author: jupin
comments.text: Laughed out loud but gave up at level 5
comments.analysis: This comment shares a personal experience but does not provide constructive feedback or insights.

comments.author: tobr
comments.text: Meanwhile, iPhone is still using this design https://xkcd.com/1884/
comments.analysis: The comment references an external source but lacks relevance to the game, making it less useful.
